zh.Whitney, Herefordshire, England: 英國英格蘭賀福爾特尼--也
zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Whitney於從1280年到1283年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾特尼--也時，她的父親Eustace de Whitney27歲，母親Mary de Mortimer23歲。她於1298年6月1日之後與Giles de Braose結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於從1321年到1325年。

zh-hans.Whitney, Herefordshire, England: 英国英格兰賀福爾特尼--也
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Whitney于从1280年到1283年出生在英国英格兰賀福爾特尼--也时，她的父亲Eustace de Whitney27岁，母亲Mary de Mortimer23岁。她于1298年6月1日之后与Giles de Braose结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于从1321年到1325年。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Whitney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Whitney in Herefordshire, the etymology of which is uncertain. The second element is Old English ēg ‘island, piece of higher ground in a low-lying area’; the first appears to be hwītan, which is either the genitive singular of an Old English byname Hwīta (meaning ‘white’), or the weak dative case (originally used after a preposition and article) of the adjective hwīt ‘white’. The name may also derive from Whitney (now Whitney Wood) in Stevenage (Hertfordshire), probably named from Old English hwītan ‘white’ + (ge)hæge ‘enclosure’.
History: John Whitney came from London, England, to Watertown, MA, in 1635, and had numerous prominent descendents.

variants.name: Witney
meanings: English: habitational name from Witney in Oxfordshire, which for centuries was a centre of the wool trade, famous in particular for the manufacture of blankets. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Witta (genitive Wittan) + Old English ēg, īeg ‘island’. There may have been some confusion with Whitney .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Whitney was born from 1280 to 1283, in Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Eustace de Whitney, was 27 and her mother, Mary de Mortimer, was 23. She married Giles de Braose after 1 June 1298. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died from 1321 to 1325.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Whitney est née du 1280 au 1283, à Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Eustace de Whitney, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Mary de Mortimer, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Giles de Braose après 1 juin 1298. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée du 1321 au 1325.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Whitney von 1280 bis 1283 in Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Eustace de Whitney 27 und ihre Mutter Mary de Mortimer 23. Sie hat nach 1. Juni 1298 Giles de Braose geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb von 1321 bis 1325.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Whitney è nata il da 1280 a 1283 nel Whitney-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Eustace de Whitney, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Mary de Mortimer, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Giles de Braose dopo il 1 giugno 1298. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì da 1321 a 1325.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Whitney(이)가 1280년 부터 1283년 까지 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 휘트니--w예에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Eustace de Whitney(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary de Mortimer(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1298년 6월 1일 이후 Giles de Braose(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1321년 부터 1325년 까지 사망했다.
